asset_records.normalized_text_snapshot:
Consider a dielectric sphere of radius a, which is uniformly polarized with polarization vector P, as shown in the figure. What is the electric field inside the sphere?
(Answer with one of: \[ -\frac{P}{5\epsilon_0} \mathbf{e}_z \], \[ -\frac{P}{4\epsilon_0} \mathbf{e}_z \], \[ -\frac{P}{3\epsilon_0} \mathbf{e}_z \], or \[ -\frac{P}{2\epsilon_0} \mathbf{e}_z \].)
